name: Coord agent detail / log (admin)
description: Admin per-agent live log view (/admin/coord/agents/[agent_id]). Like demo-detail this is a dynamic [id] route; the harness maps the `coord-agent-detail` spec id to /admin/coord/agents/<fixed sentinel id> via a routeForSpec override. ci-bot is a superuser so the page renders. The page fetches GET /operations/agent-logs/by-agent/<id> and was BROKEN before #272: a `new URL()` call threw, the catch set `error`, and the page fell to the `coord-agent-log-empty` placeholder. Post-#272 the fetch succeeds; for an unknown sentinel agent it returns zero rows, so the page renders its persistent filter chrome with NO error paragraph. We assert the agent-detail chrome that renders ONLY once the fetch resolved cleanly: the page container, the agent-id chip echoing the requested id, and the filter/refresh controls. These are distinguishable from the BROKEN state because the broken render also surfaced a 'Failed to load: …' destructive paragraph AND would red the new same-origin HTTP-500 invariant on the underlying API call — whereas the success render is 500-free with the populated header. (The sentinel agent has no logs in the ci-bot tenant, so `coord-agent-log-list` is genuinely unavailable; we assert the strongest stable populated chrome instead, per the coverage plan.) Read-only; structural only.

states.name: Agent-log header
states.description: The agent-log Card header: the 'Recent' back control returning to the agents list, and the agent-id chip echoing the requested sentinel id. The chip renders the decoded route param, confirming the dynamic [agent_id] route resolved and the component mounted.

assertions.description: Agent-id chip (data-testid coord-agent-log-agent-id) echoing the requested id.

states.name: Log filter controls
states.description: The persistent filter/refresh controls that render once the component is past its initial-loading skeleton (loading && !data renders a Skeleton; once the fetch resolves — success, even with zero rows — the controls render). The refresh button and since-select are the strongest stable signal of a cleanly-resolved fetch for a valid-but-empty agent; combined with the run-level same-origin HTTP-500 invariant (which would fire if the underlying API 500'd), this asserts the SUCCESS render, not the pre-#272 broken render.
